strengths: Current role explicitly covers AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, directly satisfying the 'experience with AI agent development' requirement
Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB/AWS CDK stack demonstrated across Optilyz (3+ years) and re:cinq, matching the primary stack closely
15+ years of polyglot fullstack delivery including Docker, Kubernetes, cloud infra, and TDD advocacy — signals strong architecture and engineering maturity
concerns: NestJS is absent from every role and project listed — a gap in the primary backend framework the team actually ships with
No explicit mention of Vitest, Jest, or Playwright despite a TDD background — modern testing toolchain fit is unverified
Kotlin/Java last appears at Altom (2016–2018) in robotics context — secondary stack knowledge is effectively dormant
response.justification: Bogdan's current re:cinq role confirms hands-on AI-agent and MCP framework work — a rare differentiator for this position. His TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS CDK experience at Optilyz maps directly onto the primary stack, and 15+ years of consistent senior delivery across diverse systems demonstrates the engineering depth the role requires. The main gaps are NestJS (not mentioned anywhere) and no named modern testing frameworks (Vitest/Jest/Playwright), which are both day-one requirements; however, given his deep Node.js background and documented TDD advocacy, these are learnable gaps rather than fundamental mismatches. Kotlin/Java knowledge appears outdated but the role lists it as secondary. Evidence-weighted, the positives on AI work and core stack alignment outweigh the framework-specific gaps.
